_comment: Default values for user-editable Bot Config settings. The dashboard reads these at init via GET /api/bot-config-defaults and uses them as the initial input value when .bot-config.json has no explicit override. The server also falls back to these when `getConfig` is asked for a value the user hasn't set. Add new user-setting defaults here rather than creating a new file per setting.
_migration: Existing one-setting-per-file tunables in this directory (e.g. ui-defaults.json, nft-providers.json) should migrate into this consolidated file over time — one setting per JSON is not our preferred pattern. Separately, most Bot Config inputs still carry their initial default as a hard-coded `value="…"` attribute in public/index.html (OOR threshold, slippage, check interval, min rebalance interval, max rebalances/day, OOR timeout, offset, etc.); those should also migrate here so operators can retune defaults without a rebuild. When migrating a key: (1) add it here, (2) update src/bot-config-defaults.js to parse + clamp it, (3) for a tunable being consolidated, delete the old JSON + loader + route entry in src/static-tunables-routes.js; for an HTML default, drop the `value=` attribute and let the /api/bot-config-defaults fetch populate the input, (4) keep the same key name so operator edits survive the move.
